description: Commander.js parent option stealing: when both the root program and a subcommand define the same option (e.g. --json), Commander recognizes the parent's option anywhere in argv by default, consuming the flag before the subcommand sees it. Subcommand opts.json is undefined even though the user passed --json after the subcommand name.
resolution: Call program.enablePositionalOptions() so parent options are only matched before the subcommand name. This lets ov --json status set the parent flag while ov status --json sets the subcommand flag.

content: Every ov CLI command must define --json as a local option on its Command instance. The parent program also defines --json (for help text and pre-subcommand usage), but enablePositionalOptions() ensures the parent does not steal the flag from subcommands. When adding a new command, always add .option('--json', 'Output as JSON') and handle it in the action handler.
